::: {.chart-card}
[Measles cases by rash onset]{.chart-title}
```{r}
outbreak_labels <- data.frame(
  week_start = as.Date(c("2025-03-30", "2026-01-11")),
  label = c("2025 TX/NM Outbreak", "2026 TX Outbreak")
) |>
  left_join(measles_dat |> select(week_start, cases), by = "week_start")

p_measles <- ggplot(measles_dat, aes(x = week_start, y = cases)) +
  geom_col(width = 5, fill = "grey30") +
  geom_text(data = outbreak_labels,
            aes(x = week_start, y = cases, label = label),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 3, color = "grey30") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Cases",
       caption = "Source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention | Weekly") +
  theme_snapshot()
plotly_chart(p_measles, links = c(
  "U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention" =
    "https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html"))
```

Confirmed measles cases in the United States by week of rash onset. Cases were
near zero for several years before a surge beginning in late 2024 linked
primarily to unvaccinated communities.
:::
